pi_items.source: NASDAQ ticker verification - Apple Inc traded under ticker AAPL on NASDAQ exchange
NinjaTrader 8 documentation confirms native Line Break chart type support. Wikipedia and StockCharts confirm Line Break charts as legitimate Japanese technical analysis tool.
Multiple sources (StockCharts, Wikipedia, financial education sites) confirm '3-Line Break' as standard configuration where reversals require price to break beyond 3 prior bars.
NinjaTrader 8 supports SMA via documented Moving Average - Simple (SMA) method. SMA is a standard technical indicator.
Period 20 is verified as standard for short-term trend analysis. Multiple sources confirm SMA 20 is commonly used for 10-20 day trading windows.
NinjaTrader 8 supports RSI via documented Relative Strength Index (RSI) method. RSI is a standard momentum oscillator developed by J. Welles Wilder.
Period 14 is verified as the default standard recommended by RSI creator J. Welles Wilder in his original 1978 specifications.
Standard NinjaTrader 8 workspace directory location confirmed across multiple environment setup scripts (setup_ninjatrader.ps1, export_result.ps1 in other tasks).
Path naming follows standard convention established in environment scripts. Directory and filename format confirmed in verifier.py constants.

pi_items.metadata_value: real Yahoo Finance data (SPY, AAPL, MSFT 2023-2024)
pi_items.verified_value: Real historical OHLCV data covering 2023-01-03 to 2024-12-31 (502 trading days)
pi_items.source: AAPL.Last.txt file contains valid historical daily OHLCV data (Open;High;Low;Close;Volume). Spot checks against historical data show prices within ~1% of official closing prices, consistent with adjusted close or intraday variation.

privileged_info_summary: Task configures a 3-Line Break chart for AAPL (Apple Inc. stock) in NinjaTrader 8 with SMA(20) and RSI(14) indicators on real historical OHLCV data spanning 502 trading days from January 3, 2023 through December 31, 2024. All metadata claims about instruments, technical indicators, charting parameters, and file paths are verified as correct. The data source is real historical price data, not synthetic.
